_comment: Golden vectors for the CANONICAL Locator serialization (Locator.canonicalJSON in vreader/Models/Locator.swift) — vreader's engine-neutral cross-device-resume contract. Sorted keys, nil omission, bookFingerprint inlined with prefix, floats '%.6f' (POSIX), \r\n/\r normalized to \n, RFC-8259 escaping. Both the Swift conformance test (the reference) and the Kotlin reference impl must produce the EXACT expectedCanonicalJSON string -> identical canonicalHash -> cross-platform position identity. cfi is intentionally absent from the canonical-resume vectors (classified platform-local->lossy-fallback in identity/locator.md; CFI dialects do not round-trip Swift-Readium <-> Kotlin-Readium).

vectors._why: EPUB position — href + progression + totalProgression are the canonical cross-device resume anchors.
TXT position — UTF-16 char offset + text quote anchor (CJK).
PDF position — page index is document-defined, canonical across renderers (PDFKit <-> PdfiumAndroid).

Canonicalization-rule coverage (Codex Gate-4): exercises RFC-8259 escaping (quote/backslash), CR-LF + bare-CR normalization to LF, and a PAIRED char range (charRangeEnd sorts before charRangeStart). textQuote input 'a"b\c' -> escaped 'a"b\c'; textContextBefore input 'p
qr' (CRLF + bare CR) -> normalized 'p
q
r'.
